claim: FEWS NET and IPC classified over 23 million people in Ethiopia, Somalia, and Kenya as Crisis or worse food insecurity during the 2020-2023 drought, the worst in 40 years, with Somalia at risk of famine.

source.title: FEWS NET East Africa food security outlook

method: Reviewed FEWS NET reports, IPC classifications, and drought impact assessments.
limitations: IPC classifications rely on available survey data, which is sparse in conflict zones.
Food-insecurity magnitude estimates have substantial uncertainty.

claim: FEWS NET documents that its food security early warning methodology combines rainfall, vegetation, market price, and livelihood data to predict food insecurity at 1-3 month lead times, with skill degrading at longer leads and for conflict-driven crises where climate signals do not dominate.

source.title: FEWS NET — Methodology for food security early warning

method: Reviewed FEWS NET methodology documentation for signal types, lead-time assumptions, and documented limitations of climate-based early warning in conflict-affected contexts.
limitations: FEWS NET methodology documentation describes operational practice, not a peer-reviewed skill assessment of forecast accuracy.
Lead-time and skill estimates for specific signal types require separate validation against historical outcomes.

Prior evidence record cited a ScienceDirect URL (pii/S2212420921001234) that returned HTTP 404 during verify:sources on 2026-06-07 and appeared to be a placeholder — replaced with this primary source.
